items.title: Diver dies after being bitten by 4.5m shark off Albany

children.content: A male diver has died after an attack by a suspected 4.5-metre shark near Michaelmas Island, off WA's Albany coast, on Saturday morning.

police albany 2
police albany

items.title: NSW woman claims ignorance of plot to smuggle fugitive bikie out of Australia

children.content: Fatma Skaf faced the Karratha Magistrates Court today over her alleged involvement in a plot to spirit Queensland bikie Dylan Cvek out of the country on a luxury yacht.

image.altText: A person wearing police/army uniform in a boat looking over at white yacht

items.title: Another pandemic 'something we need to prepare for', WA's public health chief says

children.content: Clare Huppatz, the first woman to be appointed WA's chief health officer, takes up her role at a time when Ebola, diphtheria and the flu are public health concerns.

image.altText: WA Chief Health Officer Clare Huppatz speaks to a reporter

items.title: Farmers question plan to build youth centre near Ord cotton properties

children.content: The decision to locate Kununurra's new youth centre on an abandoned farm 7km from town has sparked local concerns.

items.title: WA man charged over alleged offensive emails to federal parliamentarian

children.content: The Australian Federal Police are linking a 70-year-old man to emails sent to an electorate office after an investigation that began in April.

image.altText: An Australian Federal Police shoulder Insignia on a Blue Uniform shirt

items.title: Largest 'map of the universe' ever the latest breakthrough from Square Kilometre Array

children.content: Data gathered by scientists at a radio observatory in WA's Murchison has resulted in a new "map" detailing the magnetic web of our observable universe.

image.altText: A generated image shows cloud-like plumes of vibrant red and blue of varying brightness against a black background.

items.title: Bus driver may have had medical episode before crash that killed 61yo man

children.content: A passenger who was critically injured when a Transperth bus smashed into a tree in Perth's north dies in hospital, as police investigate whether the driver suffered a medical episode.

articles.title: Multiple people injured in bus crash, two critically, in Perth's north

children.content: A man and a woman suffer critical injuries and five other passengers are hurt as a Transperth bus crashes into a tree in Perth's north on Thursday morning.

image.altText: Police standing in front of a bus that is badly damaged after colliding with a tree

items.title: Testing underway after mass bird death in country WA

children.content: Authorities will investigate why 78 birds were found dead or dying in a Wheatbelt town, with locals speculating the cause could be related to mouse baits.

items.title: New AUKUS risk as Trump orders 'Golden Fleet' battleships

children.content: A committee of Congress wants the US Navy to explain how building "Trump-class" battleships will not delay other nuclear-powered boats, such as submarines needed for AUKUS.

image.altText: Donald Trump stands next to a posterboard showing a "Trump-class" submarine

items.title: Chalmers told tax changes threaten next wave of medical breakthroughs

children.content: Medical technology companies say new restrictions on tax refunds for research and development will hurt health startups in Australia.

image.altText: A person in a lab coat doing a test with a small tool.

items.title: Fastest-rising women's cancer in Australia still has a low profile

children.content: The incidence of uterine cancer has doubled over the past 25 years. But it's also a disease without a public profile, which means many women aren't aware of the warning signs or the risk factors.

image.altText: A composite image of a baby in a trolley next to a portrait image of a woman smiling.

items.title: The fake police scam stealing millions from Australia's Chinese community

children.content: Justin thought he was being investigated by the Chinese police, but was pulled into a vicious and frightening scam targeting the Chinese diaspora all over the world.

image.altText: A man looks worried as he speaks to someone over the phone.

items.title: Unlicensed chip factory lands WA's 'spud king' in hot water

children.content: Potato baron Tony Galati's business group has been fined $20,000 after it was found to have built an illegal Spuddies potato chip factory in WA's South West.

items.title: Hawks forward target of racist online abuse after loss to Bulldogs

children.content: The AFL and Hawthorn come out in support of Mabior Chol after the Hawks forward shares a series of abusive messages he received after Friday night's match against the Bulldogs.

items.title: Piastri struggles in Monaco F1 practice as Ferrari makes statement

children.content: Oscar Piastri has failed to make an impression in the opening practice sessions at the Monaco Grand Prix.

items.title: Ex-teacher jailed for sexually abusing children he was babysitting

children.content: Kurt David Charlton, a former WA teacher, is sentenced for sexual offences against nine victims, who were all aged under 13 at the time.

items.title: 'Very troubled project': Hospital cost blowout concerns treasurer

children.content: The new Women's and Children's Hospital was priced at $3.2 billion in 2022, before inflation and other pressures sent costs in the construction industry soaring.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit sits on a chair inside looking at the camera with a neutral expression.

items.title: Andrew made money subletting cottages on his almost rent-free estate, watchdog finds

children.content: Former prince Andrew pocketed income from cottages on the Windsor estate while living at Royal Lodge under a lease that required virtually no rent, a watchdog report has found.

image.altText: Andrew seen through the window of a car, with a red, purplish mark on his cheek.

items.title: The 20-minute coffee van that keeps a small community together

children.content: Residents of the tiny town of Underbool, in Victoria's remote Mallee region, flock to this small coffee van, which has even smaller opening hours.

image.altText: Elderly gentleman in plaid jacket and hat with hand on coffee van counter, a woman and a girl working in coffee van

items.title: How Farnsy became a figurehead for Australian protesters

children.content: Demonstrators are increasingly employing inflatable costumes and whimsy. But behind the dress up, these protesters argue it is "serious play".

image.altText: John Farnhamm with long hair and a brown leather jacket in a photo studio

items.title: Concerns raised as mining companies take local groundwater leaving towns 'vulnerable'

children.content: As a major miner seeks approval to extend its magnetite ore operations in regional Western Australia to 2048, shires are increasingly concerned about whether there's enough groundwater to go around.

items.title: Looking for another show like Off Campus? Right this way

children.content: Hannah and Garrett's steamy love story might be on ice, but we've found more romance and yearning to tick your comfort-viewing boxes.

items.title: Doctor in tears over 'atypical' moles as social media craze causes concern

children.content: Skin specialists are sounding a warning about the potential cancer risks associated with unregulated peptides at the heart of a social media-driven beauty craze.

items.title: UN envoy intervenes in Woodside gas plant challenge

children.content: The federal government's 40-year extension of Woodside's North West Shelf gas project on the Burrup Peninsula could violate human rights, an independent United Nations expert warns.
